_comment: clawdex — Codex hook wiring. install.sh writes this to ~/.codex/hooks.json (replacing HOOKS_DIR with the real install path) so the pet reacts to Codex sessions too. Codex shares Claude Code's hook contract; the `codex` arg tags these events so the daemon labels and colors Codex sessions distinctly ('repo ·cdx'). After installing, Codex prompts once to TRUST these hooks — approve it (or run codex with --dangerously-bypass-hook-trust). If your Codex build doesn't read a global ~/.codex/hooks.json, drop the same hooks block into a project's .codex/hooks.json instead.
